title: sweet gooey australian prawns dumplings
description: a crowd-pleasing australian dish featuring juniper berries and a lacquer-glaze, suitable for pescatarians.
instructions: in a large mixing bowl, combine flour, sugar, and salt.
gradually add the water, mixing until a dough forms.
add the soaked juniper berries to the dough, mixing until they are evenly distributed.
knead the dough for 10 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
cut the dough into small pieces, about the size of a small egg.
roll out each piece of dough into a small ball.
flatten each dough ball into a thin circle.
place a prawn in the center of each dough circle.
fold the dough over the prawn, forming a half-moon shape.
press the edges of the dough together to seal the dumpling.
bring a large pot of water to a boil.
gently add the dumplings to the boiling water, being careful not to overcrowd.
cook the dumplings for 5 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
remove the dumplings from the water with a slotted spoon, and drain off any excess water.
brush the dumplings with a lacquer glaze (see below) before serving.
